info.name: SuperLMS — Auth & Legal APIs
info.description:
Authentication, password management, and legal document endpoints for SuperLMS v1 API.

**Base URL variable:** `{{baseUrl}}` — set to your environment (e.g. https://superlms.in/api or http://localhost:8000/api)

**Auth variable:** `{{token}}` — captured automatically by Login requests via the test script.

request.description:
Single global login for every user type. The role is auto-detected from the `identifier`:

- **Student** → admission number (e.g. `ADM001`)
- **Teacher / Admin / Sub-admin / Accounts** → email (e.g. `admin@school.com`)

**Request body:**
- `identifier` (required, string) — admission number OR email
- `password` (required, string)

_Back-compat: `admission_number` or `email` are also accepted in place of `identifier`._

**Response:**
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "status_code": 200,
  "message": "Login successful",
  "data": {
    "user": { "id": 1, "name": "...", "email": "...", "role": "user", "user_type": "student", "organization": { ... } },
    "token": "...",
    "token_type": "Bearer",
    "role": "user",
    "user_type": "student",
    "dashboard": "student"
  }
}
```

`user_type` / `dashboard` ∈ `student | teacher | admin | accounts` — the app uses it to route to the correct dashboard.

Login as a student using admission number and password.

**Request body:**
- `admission_number` (required, string)
- `password` (required, string)

**Response:**
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "status_code": 200,
  "message": "Login successful",
  "data": {
    "user": { ... },
    "token": "...",
    "token_type": "Bearer"
  }
}
```

Login as a teacher using email and password.

**Request body:**
- `email` (required, valid email)
- `password` (required, string)

Send a 6-digit OTP to the user's email address. OTP is valid for **2 minutes**.

**Request body:**
- `email` (required, valid email)

**Response includes:**
- `user_id` — to be used in Verify OTP
- `email`
- `expires_in` (seconds)

OTP is delivered via ZeptoMail template configured in `services.zeptomail.otp_template_key`.

Verify the 6-digit OTP sent to user's email.

**Request body:**
- `user_id` (required, numeric) — from Forgot Password response
- `otp` (required, 6 digits)

After successful verification, OTP is cleared from DB and user can proceed to `change-password`.

Resend OTP to user's email. **120-second cooldown** applies between resends.

**Request body:**
- `user_id` (required, numeric)

Set new password after OTP verification (forgot password flow).

**Request body:**
- `user_id` (required, numeric)
- `password` (required, min 8 chars, must contain lowercase, uppercase, number, special char)
- `password_confirmation` (required, must match `password`)

**Important:** OTP must be verified (via `verify-otp`) before calling this. Returns a fresh auth token.

Update password while logged in. Requires current password.

**Headers:** `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`

**Request body:**
- `current_password` (required)
- `new_password` (required, same complexity rules as Change Password)
- `new_password_confirmation` (required, must match)

All existing tokens are revoked and a new one is issued.

**Authenticated (student).** Returns full student profile in 6 grouped sections:

**1. personal_info** — student_detail_id, user_id, full_name, email, mobile_number, phone, dob, gender, religion, aadhar_no, appar_id, registration_number, image, is_active, role

**2. family_info** — father_name, mother_name

**3. address_info** — local_address, permanent_address, city, state, pincode

**4. academic_info** — admission_no, date_of_admission, roll_no, board, standard_id, standard_name, standard_code, section_id, section_name

**5. transport_info** — transportation_required, active_transport (vehicle_no, route, pickup_point, is_active)

**6. organization_info** — organization_id, name, code, logo_url

**Authenticated (teacher).** Returns full teacher profile in 5 grouped sections:

**1. personal_info** — teacher_detail_id, user_id, name, email, mobile_number, phone, emergency_contact, gender, dob, image, role, is_active

**2. professional_info** — employee_id, date_of_joining, qualification, total_subjects_assigned, total_classes_assigned, total_sections_assigned

**3. address_info** — address, city, state, pincode

**4. assignments** — `subjects[]` (subject_id, subject_name, subject_code, standard_id, standard_name, section_id, section_name), `classes[]` (standard_id, standard_name, section_id, section_name), `sections[]` (section_id, section_name, standard_id, standard_name), `summary` (total_assignments, has_data)

**5. organization_info** — organization_id, name, code, logo_url

**Authenticated.** Returns app information:
- `heading`, `sub_heading`, `logo`, `address`
- `content[] { title, description }`
- `contact_details[] { type, value }`
- `core_team[] { id, name, designation, photo_url, url }` — `photo_url` is the member image; tapping a member opens `url` (falls back to `photo_url`)
- `social_media[] { platform, url, icon }`
- `documents[] { id, title, file_path, file_type }` (PDF/DOC uploads on S3)

**404** when no record exists yet — clients render a "No Data found" state instead of an error.

**Headers:** `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`

**Authenticated.** Returns school information for the logged-in user's organization:
- `about_school`, `usm_vision`, `usm_mission`, `usm_values`, `usm_goals`, `website_info`, `website_url`
- `school_mobile`, `school_email`, `school_address`
- `management_team[] { id, name, designation, photo_url, sort_order }`
- `documents[] { id, title, file_url, file_type, sort_order }`
- `organization { logo_url, name }`

**404** when no record exists — clients render a "No Data found" state.

**Headers:** `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`

**Public.** Instagram-style "Add Account" — one endpoint, both account types. The app stores the returned `{ token, account }` locally; the user can switch between stored accounts without re-login and remove any one of them.

**Unified request body (recommended):**
```json
{
  "identifier": "ADM001",
  "password": "Student@123"
}
```
`identifier` accepts a student **admission number** OR an **email** for any other role (teacher / admin / sub-admin / accounts). The server auto-detects the role by checking whether the value looks like an email address — no `login_type` needed. `user_type` ∈ `student | teacher | admin | accounts`.

**Teacher example:**
```json
{
  "identifier": "priya.mehta@superlms.in",
  "password": "Teacher@123"
}
```

**Legacy body (still accepted):**
```json
{ "login_type": "student",  "admission_number": "ADM001", "password": "..." }
{ "login_type": "teacher",  "email": "t@x.in",            "password": "..." }
```

**Response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "account": {
      "user_id": 42,
      "name": "Amit Dagur",
      "email": "amit@superlms.in",
      "role": "user",
      "role_label": "Student",
      "user_type": "student",
      "image": "https://.../avatar.jpg",
      "organization": { "id": 5, "name": "THE DEMO SCHOOL", "logo": "https://.../logo.png" },
      "class_info": { "admission_no": "ADM001", "roll_no": "01", "class_display": "Class 1 - Section A", ... }
    },
    "token":      "abc123...",
    "token_type": "Bearer"
  }
}
```
For a teacher, `user_type` is `"teacher"` and `class_info` carries `{ employee_id, qualification, class_display }`.

**App flow (Instagram-style):**
1. User taps **Add Account** in the switcher and enters admission no / email + password.
2. App calls this endpoint.
3. App appends the returned `{ token, account }` to its local accounts list and shows it as a card with: image, name, `user_type` badge.
4. To switch, the app loads the stored token for the chosen account and uses it as the Bearer.
5. To remove an account, send `POST /remove` with that account's token (see next request) — only that token is revoked, the other accounts keep working.

**Authenticated.** Returns compact profile snapshot for the currently authenticated user.

**Use case:** App periodically calls this for each stored token to refresh the snapshot (so the switcher shows the latest name / image / class after admin updates).

**Returns:** same `snapshot` shape as Add Account response.

**Authenticated.** Revokes ONLY the current access token (the one in the Authorization header).

**Use case:** User removes an account from the device switcher. App sends this with the token-to-remove in Authorization, server revokes only that token (other linked accounts keep working).

**App flow:**
1. User taps the X / Remove icon on an account card in switcher
2. App calls this with `Authorization: Bearer <that-account-token>`
3. On success, app deletes the stored `{ token, snapshot }` entry locally

Note: tries `{{added_token}}` for testing — switch this header to the specific account's token in production.

**Authenticated.** Auto-discovers all accounts sharing the current user's email + role across organizations.

Useful when a user (same email) is enrolled in multiple schools — they can see all linked schools without manually adding each one.

**Returns:** array of `{ user_id, organization_id, is_current, school: { id, name, logo, active } }`

**Authenticated.** Revokes current token and issues a new one for the same user-email in a different organization (used with the auto-discovery flow `/switch-account/schools`).

**Body:** `{ organization_id }`

**Returns:** new token + user data.

Student raises a query/contact request to the school admin.

**Body:** `{ topic, student_query }` (optional `image` as multipart, max 2MB jpeg/png/jpg/gif)
Returns the student's own contact requests with admin replies.
Student adds a follow-up reply to an existing contact thread.

Teacher raises a query/contact request to the school admin.

**Body:** `{ topic, teacher_query }` (optional `image` as multipart, max 2MB jpeg/png/jpg/gif)
Returns the teacher's own contact requests with admin replies.
Teacher adds a follow-up reply to an existing contact thread.
Lists announcements visible to the caller (filtered by their organization, class, section based on role).
Returns a single announcement with full description, attachments, and target audience.

Lists published exams for the caller's school.

**Optional filters:** `academic_year`, `exam_type`, `search`, `per_page`

Returns paginated list with status (`upcoming`/`ongoing`/`completed`).

Returns full exam detail + role-scoped syllabus embedded.

**Syllabus scoping:**
- Student → syllabus for their class+section only
- Teacher → syllabus for their assigned (standard, subject) pairs

Syllabus is grouped by (standard, subject) with chapters and topics.

Returns only the syllabus chapters+topics for an exam, scoped to the caller.

**Optional query:** `subject_id` to filter to one subject.

Returns: `[{ standard_id, standard_name, subject_id, subject_name, chapter_count, chapters: [{ id, name, description, order, topics: [{ id, topic_name }] }] }]`

item.name: 9. Books
item.description:
Class library (PDF books).

**Auto-scoped by role** (same endpoint, both roles):
- **Student** → books for their class + section (and class-wide books with `section_id=NULL`).
- **Teacher** → books for `(class, subject)` pairs derived from their **timetable**.

List response carries `pdf_url` too — the card 'open' action does NOT need a separate detail call.

**Card field mapping:**
- Student card → `title` (book name), `cover_url` (cover image), `subject.name` → tap = open `pdf_url`.
- Teacher card → `title`, `cover_url`, `subject.name`, `standard.name` (class), `section.name` → tap = open `pdf_url`.

`logo_url` is a back-compat alias of `cover_url`.

request.description:
Lists books auto-scoped by caller's role.

- **Student** → books for their class + section (also picks up class-wide books where `section_id IS NULL`).
- **Teacher** → books for the `(class, subject)` pairs they teach (derived from `teacher_time_tables`).

**Optional filters:** `standard_id`, `section_id`, `subject_id`, `search`, `per_page`.

**Each item:**
```json
{
  "id": 14,
  "title": "NCERT Mathematics — Class 8",
  "cover_url": "https://.../math-8.png",
  "logo_url":  "https://.../math-8.png",
  "standard": { "id": 8,  "name": "Class 8" },
  "section":  { "id": 24, "name": "A" },
  "subject":  { "id": 3,  "name": "Mathematics" },
  "pdf_url":  "https://.../math-8.pdf"
}
```

**Use:** student/teacher app library screen. Tap on a card → open `pdf_url` directly (no extra detail call needed).

response.name: Sample — Student (role=user)
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Books fetched successfully.",
  "data": [
    {
      "id": 14,
      "title": "NCERT Mathematics — Class 8",
      "cover_url": "https://superlms-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/admin/books/covers/math-8.png",
      "logo_url":  "https://superlms-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/admin/books/covers/math-8.png",
      "standard": { "id": 8, "name": "Class 8" },
      "section":  { "id": 24, "name": "A" },
      "subject":  { "id": 3, "name": "Mathematics" },
      "pdf_url":  "https://superlms-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/admin/books/pdfs/math-8.pdf"
    },
    {
      "id": 15,
      "title": "NCERT Science — Class 8",
      "cover_url": "https://superlms-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/admin/books/covers/sci-8.png",
      "logo_url":  "https://superlms-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/admin/books/covers/sci-8.png",
      "standard": { "id": 8, "name": "Class 8" },
      "section":  null,
      "subject":  { "id": 4, "name": "Science" },
      "pdf_url":  "https://superlms-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/admin/books/pdfs/sci-8.pdf"
    }
  ],
  "meta": { "current_page": 1, "per_page": 20, "total": 6, "last_page": 1 }
}

response.name: Sample — Teacher (role=teacher)
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Books fetched successfully.",
  "data": [
    {
      "id": 14,
      "title": "NCERT Mathematics — Class 8",
      "cover_url": "https://superlms-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/admin/books/covers/math-8.png",
      "logo_url":  "https://superlms-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/admin/books/covers/math-8.png",
      "standard": { "id": 8, "name": "Class 8" },
      "section":  { "id": 24, "name": "A" },
      "subject":  { "id": 3, "name": "Mathematics" },

request.description:
Returns a single book (same shape as a list item), still role-scoped.

Mostly **optional** for the normal list flow — list response already carries `pdf_url`. Use this for **deep-link / share-link / push-notification** scenarios where the front-end only has a book id, not a list.

- **200** → full book payload
- **404** → book outside the caller's scope or doesn't exist (security check — a teacher cannot deep-link to another class's book).

item.name: 10. Teacher → Marks & Exam Copies
item.description:
Teacher app APIs for managing student marks and uploading exam-copy PDFs.

All endpoints are auto-scoped to the (class, section, subject) triples the teacher teaches via the timetable. 403 if they touch a class+subject they don't teach.

request.description:
Every distinct (class, section, subject) the authenticated teacher teaches.

Each item carries a ready-to-display `label` (e.g. `"Class 8 · A · Mathematics"`) plus the three IDs for follow-up calls.

**Use:** populate the "select class + subject" dropdown on the marks / exam-copy upload screens.

response.name: Sample 200

request.description: Students enrolled in the chosen (class, section). 403 if the teacher doesn't teach this class+section.
Paginated list of marks rows the teacher can see (scoped to their class+subject triples).
Upload marks for one student. 403 if the teacher doesn't teach that triple, 409 if marks already exist (use update).
Partial update — only sent fields change. Percentage is recomputed.
Only rows that have a PDF attached. Each item carries `pdf_url`.
**multipart/form-data**. PDF required, max 10 MB. Optional marks fields save the marks on the same row.

formdata.description: Optional — include to replace existing PDF.

request.description: Optional PDF replacement + any metadata field. Send `_method=PUT` since the file upload needs POST.
Removes the row and wipes the PDF on S3.

item.name: 11. Student → Marks & Exam Copies
item.description:
Student app APIs for viewing own marks, overall performance, and exam-copy PDFs.

Read-only — always scoped to the authenticated student.

request.description: Paginated marks list. Each row: `exam_name, subject_name, marks_obtained, max_marks, percentage, grade` — exactly what a marks-list card needs.
Aggregate dashboard: `overall_percentage`, `subject_wise_performance`, `performance_trend` (last 5), `grade_distribution`, `total_exams_given`.
Only rows where a teacher has uploaded a PDF. Each item carries `pdf_url` — front-end card tap → open PDF.
404 if the id doesn't belong to the caller.

Returns classes (standards) the caller can see.

- **Student** → just their own class
- **Teacher** → only classes they teach
- **Admin** → all classes in org

Response: `[{ id, name, code, order }]`

Returns sections of a given class (role-scoped).

**Query:** `standard_id` (required)

Response: `[{ id, name, standard_id }]`

Returns subjects for a class (and optionally section), role-scoped.

**Query:** `standard_id` (required), `section_id` (optional)

- Without `section_id`: uses `standard_subjects` mapping
- With `section_id`: uses `section_subjects` mapping

Response: `[{ id, name }]`

Returns published exams for filter dropdowns.

**Optional query:** `academic_year`

Response: `[{ id, name, type, academic_year, start_date, end_date }]`
Calendar events for the caller's organization (optionally filtered by month/date range). Same for student & teacher.
Events scheduled for today.
Single calendar event detail.
All subjects for the caller (role-scoped).
Subjects assigned to the authenticated teacher.
List syllabus entries (filter by class/section/subject).
Upload a syllabus document (PDF/doc).
Update a syllabus entry / replace its file.
Delete a syllabus entry.
Get a downloadable URL for the syllabus file.
List chapters with their topics for a class/subject.
Create a chapter and its topics.
Rename a chapter.
Delete a chapter (and its topics).
Update a topic name.
Delete a topic.
List quiz questions with options (filterable by class/section/chapter/topic).
Create a quiz question with options. **Teacher.**
**Teacher only.** Update question/fields; if `options` provided they fully replace existing options. Partial fields allowed.
**Teacher only.** Deletes the question, its options, and any submitted answers.
Student submits an answer to a quiz question.
Get the caller's submitted answers / results.
List homework (teacher view, filterable).
Homework for the logged-in student (their class/section).
Single homework detail with attachments.
**Teacher.** Create homework (optional attachment).
**Teacher.** Update homework fields/attachment.
**Teacher.** Delete homework.

Self attendance view for **both roles**. Optional `month=YYYY-MM` (defaults current). Returns day-wise statuses (present/absent/holiday) + monthly summary (total/working/present/absent days + percentage). Unmarked days = holiday.
**Teacher.** Students of a class/section with any existing marks for the date.
**Teacher only.** Bulk mark student attendance (status 1=present, 0=absent).
**Teacher.** Class attendance summary for a date.
Teacher's own attendance records.
Teacher's attendance status for today.

Public endpoint. Returns Terms and Conditions: `platform_logo`, `platform_name`, `company_name`, `company_cin`, `metadata { sections[] {head, desc}, files[] {title, file_path, file_type}, additional_info[] {key, value} }`, `last_updated`.

**404** when not configured — clients render a "No Data found" state.

Public endpoint. Returns Privacy Policy: `metadata { sections[] {head, desc} }`, `last_updated`.

**404** when not configured — clients render a "No Data found" state.

Public endpoint. Returns Terms of Use: `metadata { sections[] {head, desc} }`, `last_updated`.

**404** when not configured — clients render a "No Data found" state.

**Authenticated.** Returns rules and regulations for the logged-in user's organization.

**Headers:** `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`

**Response fields:**
- `id` — record id
- `organization_id` — org this belongs to
- `sections[]` — array of rule sections (title, body, etc. as set by admin)
- `additional_info[]` — extra metadata fields
- `files[]` — uploaded documents `{ title, file_path (S3 URL), file_type, file_size (bytes) }`
- `last_updated` — when admin last updated the content
- `created_at`, `updated_at`

**404** when not configured — clients render a "No Data found" state.

Lists instructors auto-scoped by user role:
- **Student** (`role=user`) -> only instructors who teach the student's `(standard, section)` via the **timetable** (`teacher_time_tables`). Each instructor's `subjects[]` is scoped to the subjects they teach that student's class.
- **Teacher / Admin** -> all active instructors in the organization, with `subjects`, `classes`, and `assigned_classes` collections.

**Optional filters:** `subject_id`, `search`, `per_page`. `standard_id` is ignored for students (they're already auto-scoped).

**Student response item:** `{ id, name, email, avatar, employee_id, subjects: [{ id, name, code, image }] }`

Single instructor (role-scoped) including profile fields: `qualification`, `date_of_joining`, `phone`, `city`, `state`, `address`.

Returns **404** if the instructor is outside the caller's scope - e.g. a student requesting an instructor who does not teach their class via the timetable.

**Authenticated (student).** One-shot home: `summary`, full `academic` block (structures, totals, upcoming installments, paid slips, penalty), full `transport` block (route, 12-month schedule, totals, upcoming, paid slips) or null, and `overall_payments` (merged academic+transport feed).
**Authenticated (student).** Academic: structures, totals, `upcoming` installments (cycle-based, overdue penalty + payable), `paid` slips, and penalty policy.
**Authenticated (student).** Only penalties charged to the student, each tied to the payment (receipt) it was levied on. Returns penalty_per_day, total_penalty, count, items[].
**Authenticated (student).** Overall fee summary totals.
**Authenticated (student).** Fee structure items for the student's class.
**Authenticated (student).** Paginated list of the student's recorded payments. Query: `fee_type` (academic|transport), `per_page`.

**Authenticated (student). Rate-limited (5/min).** Creates a PENDING transaction and returns a PhonePe hosted-checkout `redirect_url` + `merchant_order_id`. Open the URL in a browser/WebView; the app polls status on return. `fee_type`: academic|transport.
**Authenticated (student).** Polls the latest payment state. On COMPLETED, the matching fee_payments record is created exactly once. Returns state (PENDING|COMPLETED|FAILED), paid, receipt_number.

**Public (PhonePe server-to-server).** No bearer auth. PhonePe sends an `Authorization` header = SHA256(webhook_username:webhook_password); the server verifies it before settling. Configure the URL + username/password under PhonePe Developer Settings -> Webhooks.

**Authenticated (student).** Transport route + 12-month schedule (paid/partial/pending with carry-forward), totals (monthly/annual/paid/remaining), upcoming months, and paid slips. 404 if no transport assigned.

**Authenticated (student). Rate-limited.** Transport online payment. `months` = which months the student intends to clear (apr..mar keys); `transportation_id` optional (resolves active route). Amount is editable — excess carries forward to next months via oldest-first allocation. On success a transport_fee_payments row is created (visible in admin + 12-month schedule).
**Public. Rate-limited (login).** School admin login (role admin / sub-admin), email + password. Returns `{ user (with organization), token, token_type }`. Set the returned token into `{{token}}`.
**Authenticated (admin/sub-admin).** Current admin profile + organization.
**Authenticated (admin/sub-admin).** Headline counts: students, teachers, fees collected (this month + total).
**Public. Rate-limited (login).** Accounts-staff login (role accounts), email + password. Returns `{ user (with organization), token, token_type }`. Set the returned token into `{{token}}`.
**Authenticated (accounts).** Current accounts profile + organization.
**Authenticated (accounts).** Finance numbers: fees collected (today/month/total), transport collected, pending dues, students.
